subject: Biology
title: Egg and Sperm: Cells of Creation
description: One-minute skill: Egg and Sperm: Cells of Creation.

hook.text: The human egg cell is the body's largest—visible to the naked eye at 0.1 millimeters.

buildup.text: Sperm cells are tiny, stripped to just DNA, a tail, and mitochondria for fuel.

discovery.text: When sperm meets egg, two half-sets of chromosomes unite into a full set of 46.

twist.text: Of roughly 250 million sperm released, typically only one fertilizes the egg.

climax.text: That single fertilized cell divides into every cell type in a human body.

punchline.text: Two half-cells make a whole new person.

quiz.question: What is the largest cell in the human body?
